paper_metadata.title: A Generalist Agent
paper_metadata.abstract: Inspired by progress in large-scale language modeling, we apply a similar approach towards building a single generalist agent beyond the realm of text outputs. The agent, which we refer to as Gato, works as a multi-modal, multi-task, multi-embodiment generalist policy. The same network with the same weights can play Atari, caption images, chat, stack blocks with a real robot arm and much more, deciding based on its context whether to output text, joint torques, button presses, or other tokens. In this report we describe the model and the data, and document the current capabilities of Gato.

extraction_data.innovation: Gato is a generalist agent that uses a single transformer-based neural network with the same weights to perform a wide range of tasks across different modalities and embodiments, including dialogue, image captioning, robotics, and gaming. The model serializes all data into a flat sequence of tokens, enabling unified processing akin to large-scale language models.
extraction_data.pretraining_task: Supervised learning on serialized multi-modal sequences
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: Multi-modal dataset including images, text, proprioception, joint torques, button presses, and other discrete and continuous observations and actions
extraction_data.finetuning_task: Fine-tuning on task-specific demonstrations for out-of-distribution tasks
extraction_data.extension: Tokenization scheme for multi-modal data, including SentencePiece for text, image patching for images, and mu-law encoding for continuous values, enabling unified sequence processing across modalities.
extraction_data.research_problem: Generalist multimodal agent capable of performing diverse tasks across domains (robotics, vision, language) with a single model
extraction_data.application: Dialogue, image captioning, robotics (e.g., stacking blocks with a real robot arm), Atari games, simulated 3D environment navigation, instruction following
